glossary.meaning: customer_id with ≥1 delivered order in period
glossary.note: ≠ registered customer (has account, may never ordered)

glossary.meaning: first ever delivered order falls in queried period
glossary.sql: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY day) = 1 on delivered orders

glossary.meaning: placed a delivered order before current period
count of distinct delivered orders in a period
glossary.note: ≠ transaction (which is a session-level event metric); always filter order_status='delivered'

glossary.meaning: total order value billed to the customer before any deductions
glossary.note: ≠ net GMV; maps to restaurant_gmv column in daily_orders

glossary.meaning: GMV after deducting noon-borne discounts and promotions
glossary.note: ≠ gross GMV; maps to commissionable_gmv column in daily_orders

glossary.meaning: total noon-funded promotional discount on delivered orders in a period
glossary.note: clarify whether to include partner-funded discounts or noon-funded only before querying; confirm exact column name in daily_orders

glossary.meaning: average delivered orders per unique active customer in a period
glossary.note: always computed on delivered orders only
glossary.sql: COUNT(DISTINCT order_nr) / COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) WHERE order_status='delivered'

glossary.meaning: ratio of transacting sessions to outlet-visit sessions; measures menu-open to order conversion
glossary.note: numerator and denominator both use COUNT(DISTINCT session_id); never mix with order-level counts
glossary.sql: COUNT(DISTINCT session_id WHERE event_type='transaction_v2') / COUNT(DISTINCT session_id WHERE event_type='page_outlet_v2')

glossary.meaning: one app open = one session, id=sid
is_bounce<>1 AND is_moved<>1
glossary.note: bounce/move flags in session_segments_nat NOT nat_events_complete

glossary.meaning: is_bounce=1 in session_segments_nat
status='active' AND is_consumer_visible='1' AND outlet_deleted=0
open_app→page_outlet→add_to_cart→page_checkout→transaction
glossary.note: for pre-agg hub/city funnel use daily_hubz_rca_nat

glossary.meaning: VIP loyalty subscriber; is_vip_customer=true in daily_orders
delivered order in observation window but 0 delivered orders in next 30d
≥4 delivered orders in last 30d
geo sub-division within city e.g. DXB-HUB1; column=Customer_hub in daily_orders, Hub in daily_hubz_rca_nat
non-food to food session; customer came from noon marketplace to food app
session from CRM-triggered comms (push/email/sms)
days between consecutive delivered orders per customer
glossary.sql: DATE_DIFF(order_date, DATE(LAG(order_placed_at) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_placed_at)), DAY)

glossary.meaning: daily_orders→country='ae'; nat_events_complete→locale LIKE '%ae%' — DO NOT MIX
ultra-fast ~15min delivery via Flash service
Partner ID — maps to the column id_partner_owner in daily_orders and outlet_status_complete. Always filter using id_partner_owner = <value> (integer). NEVER use restaurant_code for a PID filter.
glossary.note: PID ≠ restaurant_code. PID is id_partner_owner. One PID can own multiple restaurant_codes/brands.

glossary.meaning: Restaurant ID — numeric identifier mapping to id_restaurant in outlet_status_complete and temp_outlet_status. daily_orders does NOT have id_restaurant directly; to filter daily_orders by RID, JOIN outlet_status_complete on restaurant_code and filter by id_restaurant. NEVER use restaurant_code value as a RID substitute.
glossary.note: RID ≠ restaurant_code. RID is numeric id_restaurant. restaurant_code is a string join key.
glossary.sql: JOIN noonbifood.reporting.outlet_status_complete osc ON do.restaurant_code = osc.restaurant_code WHERE osc.id_restaurant = <rid_value>

glossary.meaning: parent brand identified by restaurant_code; one restaurant has many outlets; count via COUNT(DISTINCT restaurant_code)
individual location identified by Outlet_Code; sub-unit of a restaurant; count via COUNT(DISTINCT Outlet_Code)
courier logistics via Ping service; may be non-food
customizable meal plan/bundle via Mix product
glossary.sql: COUNT(DISTINCT order_nr) WHERE order_status='delivered' FROM noonbifood.growthv1.mealplan_daily_orders
glossary.note: ALWAYS use noonbifood.growthv1.mealplan_daily_orders for Mix product queries — do NOT filter daily_orders. When user says 'mix performance', 'mix analysis', or just 'mix', they mean this specific product.

glossary.meaning: $.oc=outlet_code, $.wt=widget_type path (format: page/widget/subtype), $.szf=food zone flag, $.mpe=marketplace entry (food=food orders), $.wt[0]=page, $.wt[1]=widget
user funnel events in raw_events_v2: outlet_impression_v2, page_outlet_v2, transaction_v2, place_click_v2, page_checkout_v2, add_to_cart_v2, item_click_v2, item_impression, place_order_v2
COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) WHERE event_type='transaction_v2' — never COUNT(*) or COUNT(DISTINCT uid)
COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) WHERE event_type='outlet_impression_v2' — sessions that saw an outlet
COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) WHERE event_type='page_outlet_v2' — sessions that opened an outlet page
COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) WHERE event_type='item_impression'
COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) WHERE event_type='item_click_v2'
COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) WHERE event_type='add_to_cart_v2'
COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) WHERE event_type='page_checkout_v2'
COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) WHERE event_type='place_order_v2'
COUNT(DISTINCT $.oc) WHERE event_type='outlet_impression_v2' — unique outlets impressed, not sessions
COUNT(DISTINCT $.oc) WHERE event_type='page_outlet_v2' — unique outlets visited
COUNT(DISTINCT $.oc) WHERE event_type='transaction_v2' — unique outlets that got a transaction
Rider on Demand — an on-demand delivery rider model where riders are dispatched dynamically based on real-time order demand

BOTH nat_events_complete AND raw_events_v2 use _v2 suffixed names: outlet_impression_v2, page_outlet_v2, transaction_v2, place_click_v2, page_checkout_v2, add_to_cart_v2, item_click_v2, place_order_v2. Exception: item_impression has NO _v2 suffix. Always use suffixed names on nat_events_complete. Full canonical event list: ('outlet_impression_v2','page_outlet_v2','transaction_v2','place_click_v2','page_checkout_v2','add_to_cart_v2','item_click_v2','item_impression','place_order_v2')
